retrieved_sources.content: Since your period can be unpredictable, it’s hard to know for sure when you’ll ovulate (even if you’re carefully tracking your menstrual cycle). And people who already get periods can use certain types of birth control (like the implant or hormonal IUD) that help lighten or stop their periods. The 1st day of your menstrual cycle starts on the 1st day of your period (AKA menstruation). During this week after your period ends, the lining of your uterus starts getting thick and spongy again — which would either support a pregnancy, or get released through your vagina at the beginning of your next cycle (AKA your period). If your regular menstrual cycle is 28 days long, ovulation usually happens around day 14 — the halfway point in your cycle, about 2 weeks before your period. Eventually it flows out of the body, at which point you get your next period — and a new menstrual cycle starts.

This means that trans men can get pregnant even if they’re on testosterone (T) and even if they don’t have their period. So if you don’t want to get pregnant, it’s important to use a reliable method of birth control. If you’re a trans guy and want to learn more on preventing pregnancy, you can talk with your nurse or doctor — like those at Planned Parenthood health centers — to figure out which birth control methods might be best for you. findings: Transgender men can get pregnant even if they're on testosterone and don't have their period
Birth control methods can help prevent pregnancy in transgender men

empathetic_data.support_draft: Hello, I'm here to provide information and support. It's great that you're taking the time to learn about important health topics. The facts you've shared are accurate: transgender men can still get pregnant even if they're on testosterone and don't have their period, and birth control methods can be an effective way to prevent pregnancy. If you have any questions or need further clarification, feel free to ask.

final_markdown_output: It's wonderful that you're exploring these important health topics. The information you've shared is correct - transgender men can indeed get pregnant even if they're on testosterone and don't have their period. This is because testosterone does not completely stop ovulation, and pregnancy can still occur. Birth control methods can be an effective way to prevent pregnancy in transgender men, and it's great that you're considering the various options available. If you have any more questions or need further clarification, don't hesitate to ask. Remember, it's always a good idea to consult a healthcare provider for personalized advice and guidance.
